CONSTITUTION OF THE MONTANA ASSOCIATION OF DISABILITY EXAMINERS (MONTADE).

ARTICLE I

Name

The professional, fraternal and non-profit name of this organization shall be
MONTADE, standing for the Montana Association of Disability Examiners.

ARTICLE II

Purpose

1. To bring together in a professional and fraternal manner those individuals
   engaged in the documentation and evaluation of medical and/or vocational
   information submitted in support of disability claims.

II. To provide a forum for the discussion of factors related to the documentation,
evaluation and adjudication of disability claims.

III. To promote a better understanding by the general public and other concerned
professionals of the documentary needs and methods employed in the total
evaluation of disability claims.

IV. To provide a forum to project the professional goals of the membership.

ARTICLE III

Affiliation

1. The Montana Association of Disability Examiners, MONTADE is a chapter of
   the National Association of Disability Examiners, (NADE) and subject to
   the provisions of the constitution and by-laws pertaining to National
   Association of Disability Examiners.

II. The constitution of the Montana Association of Disability Examiners, MONTADE,
shall not be contradictory to the National Association of Disability Examiners
(NADE).

ARTICLE IV

Definitions

I. "Association" means the National Association of Disability Examiners

II. "Chapter" means MONTADE, The Montana Association of Disability Examiners.

1. "Montana" is the geographic area of the Great State of Montana served by
   MONTADE.

ARTICLE V

Membership

I. Members must be members of the National Association of Disability Examiners

page 2

and be involved in the documentation and/or evaluation of medical and/or vocational information in disability claims.

- II. Associate members may be other governmental, private, medical, or non-profit employers or employees who use or provide medical and vocational information of salience to the processing of disability claims.

ARTICLE VI

By-laws

MONTADE will develop, adopt, and adapt by-laws consistent with this document.

ARTICLE VII

Officers

The officers of MONTADE shall be a president, president elect and secretary-treasurer.
They shall be elected annually for terms of one year and may not serve in the same
office for more than two consecutive terms. Their duties shall be those customarily ascribed to such positions, including specific duties assigned to each officer as specified in the by-laws of MONTADE.

ARTICLE VIII

Executive Board

The executive board shall consist of the elected officers of MONTADE currently serving, the immediate past president, the president elect, and any committee chairman appointed by the president.

ARTICLE IX

Meetings

I. MONTADE shall conduct monthly meetings of the membership.

II. The president may call a special meeting when deemed necessary and with proper advance notification to the membership.

ARTICLE X

Amendments

The MONTADE constitution may be amended as follows:

1. An amendment may be proposed by a majority of the total executive board and ratified by two-thirds vote of the MONTADE members present and voting at an official meeting, or;

II. Any MONTADE member may propose an amendment at the annual meeting, to be
voted upon by the members at that meeting. It shall be considered ratified by two-thirds of the members present who vote in favor.

ARTICLE XI

Limitations

The MONTADE shall not carryon any activities not permitted to be carried on

by a corporation exempt from Federal Income Tax under provisions of the Internal Revenue Code.

1. An amendment may be proposed by a majority of the
   Regional Board and ratified by a two-thirds (2/3)
   majority of the Convention of Delegates.
2. An amendment may be proposed by any member of the Convention
   of Delegates at its annual meeting. If an amendment so
   proposed is approved by a simple majority of the Convention
   of Delegates, it shall be referred for study to a special
   committee appointed by the President. This committee shall
   poll the membership of the Region and report to the Assembly
   at its next annual meeting. If such proposed amendment

is ratified by a two-thirds (2/3) majority of those present
and voting, it shall become effective immediately.

1. No amendment may be proposed that would be in conflict with
   the Constitution and by-laws of the National Association of
   Disability Examiners.

Revised and adopted May, 1985.
